prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_objective primary_actor_slot_obj)
(reserve_faction_option primary_actor_slot_obj faction_option_obj_1)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(commit_loadout_to_objective primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(stage_primary_slot_with_consumable primary_actor_slot_obj route_option_a_obj consumable_item_obj)
(finalize_primary_slot_preparation primary_actor_slot_obj route_option_a_obj loadout_option_obj consumable_item_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(initialize_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj)
(reserve_faction_option secondary_actor_slot_obj faction_option_obj_2)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(commit_loadout_to_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(stage_secondary_slot_with_consumable secondary_actor_slot_obj route_option_b_obj consumable_item_obj)
(finalize_secondary_slot_preparation secondary_actor_slot_obj route_option_b_obj loadout_option_obj consumable_item_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(assemble_mission_standard primary_actor_slot_obj secondary_actor_slot_obj route_option_a_obj route_option_b_obj mission_instance_obj)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(mark_mission_node_processed mission_instance_obj primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(propagate_deployment_to_primary_slot primary_actor_slot_obj mission_instance_obj)
(propagate_deployment_to_secondary_slot secondary_actor_slot_obj mission_instance_obj)
(apply_salvage_token_to_objective primary_actor_slot_obj mission_token_obj loadout_option_obj)
(reintegrate_primary_slot_via_faction_option primary_actor_slot_obj faction_option_obj_1 mission_token_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective primary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(apply_salvage_token_to_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj mission_token_obj loadout_option_obj)
(reintegrate_secondary_slot_via_faction_option secondary_actor_slot_obj faction_option_obj_2 mission_token_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective secondary_actor_slot_obj loadout_option_obj)
(initialize_objective agent_profile_obj_1)
(reserve_faction_option agent_profile_obj_1 faction_option_obj_1)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective agent_profile_obj_1 loadout_option_obj)
(commit_loadout_to_objective agent_profile_obj_1 loadout_option_obj)
(install_upgrade_module_on_mission_node agent_profile_obj_1 upgrade_module_obj_1 mission_instance_obj)
(finalize_agent_node_upgrade agent_profile_obj_1 upgrade_module_obj_1 mission_instance_obj loadout_option_obj)
(attach_equipment_to_agent agent_profile_obj_1 equipment_attachment_obj)
(assign_support_to_objective agent_profile_obj_1 support_unit_obj)
(flag_agent_for_promotion agent_profile_obj_1 equipment_attachment_obj support_unit_obj upgrade_module_obj_1 mission_instance_obj)
(assign_leadership_trait_to_agent agent_profile_obj_1 leadership_trait_obj)
(prepare_agent_with_trait agent_profile_obj_1 leadership_trait_obj upgrade_module_obj_1 mission_instance_obj)
(finalize_agent_promotion_no_modifier agent_profile_obj_1)
(detach_equipment_from_agent agent_profile_obj_1 equipment_attachment_obj)
(unassign_support_from_objective agent_profile_obj_1 support_unit_obj)
(remove_leadership_trait_from_agent agent_profile_obj_1 leadership_trait_obj)
(apply_salvage_token_to_objective agent_profile_obj_1 mission_token_obj loadout_option_obj)
(reintegrate_agent_with_faction_option agent_profile_obj_1 faction_option_obj_1 mission_token_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective agent_profile_obj_1 loadout_option_obj)
(initialize_objective agent_profile_obj_2)
(reserve_faction_option agent_profile_obj_2 faction_option_obj_2)
(reserve_loadout_for_objective agent_profile_obj_2 loadout_option_obj)
(commit_loadout_to_objective agent_profile_obj_2 loadout_option_obj)
(install_upgrade_module_on_mission_node agent_profile_obj_2 upgrade_module_obj_2 mission_instance_obj)
(finalize_agent_node_upgrade agent_profile_obj_2 upgrade_module_obj_2 mission_instance_obj loadout_option_obj)
(attach_equipment_to_agent agent_profile_obj_2 equipment_attachment_obj)
(assign_support_to_objective agent_profile_obj_2 support_unit_obj)
(flag_agent_for_promotion agent_profile_obj_2 equipment_attachment_obj support_unit_obj upgrade_module_obj_2 mission_instance_obj)
(assign_leadership_trait_to_agent agent_profile_obj_2 leadership_trait_obj)
(prepare_agent_with_trait agent_profile_obj_2 leadership_trait_obj upgrade_module_obj_2 mission_instance_obj)
(finalize_agent_promotion_no_modifier agent_profile_obj_2)
(detach_equipment_from_agent agent_profile_obj_2 equipment_attachment_obj)
(unassign_support_from_objective agent_profile_obj_2 support_unit_obj)
(remove_leadership_trait_from_agent agent_profile_obj_2 leadership_trait_obj)
(apply_salvage_token_to_objective agent_profile_obj_2 mission_token_obj loadout_option_obj)
(reintegrate_agent_with_faction_option agent_profile_obj_2 faction_option_obj_2 mission_token_obj)
(release_loadout_from_objective agent_profile_obj_2 loadout_option_obj)
```

## Explanation of the Plan

The provided plan systematically achieves the goal by first preparing and salvaging the slots to make the mission ready and processed, then processing both agents through their node upgrades, promotions, salvages, and reintegrations. Key resource management includes:
- Reusing the single loadout by reserving, using, and releasing as needed.
- Using the consumable path for both slots, which releases the consumable after finalization.
- Reusing the salvage token via reintegration.
- Reusing support unit, equipment, and leadership trait by unassigning/detaching after use for the first agent.
- Reusing faction options (f1 and f2) after slot reintegrations.

This sequence ensures all preconditions are met without conflicts, leveraging the standard mission assembly path (no asset/modifier requirements) for simplicity. The plan is complete and minimal for the given constraints.
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL, and I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions.


## Examining the Planning Problem  
- The task involves creating a plan in PDDL, focusing on output as a sequence of ground actions.  
- The goal centers on making primary_actor_slot_obj with secondary_actor.

## Examining the Initial State  
- Initial state includes factions 1,2,3, loadout_option_obj, support_unit_obj, and consumable_item_obj ready for use.  
- Routes assigned are route_option_a_obj for primary and route_option_b_obj for secondary, with mission_instance_obj and upgrades upgrade_module_obj_1 and _2 available.  

## Analyzing Agent Assignments  
- Agent_profile_obj_1 and agent_profile_obj_2 are both assigned to primary_actor_slot_obj and secondary_actor_slot_obj, which seems unusual.  
- Agents have upgrades: agent_profile_obj_1 with um1 and agent_profile_obj_2 with um2, and are bound to faction markers fm1 and fm2 respectively.  

## Exploring Deployment Readiness  
- Goal is to make primary_actor_slot_obj, secondary_actor_slot_obj, agent_profile_obj_1, and agent_profile_obj_2 ready_for_deployment.  
- Ready_for_deployment requires reintegration actions, which need entity_salvaged, reserved_faction, and bound_salvage_token, none of which are initially present.  

## Checking Progression Steps  
- To achieve entity_salvaged, entities need entity_promotion_applied, then apply_salvage_token_to_objective, and reserve loadout.  
- The domain suggests a progression involving lockout recovery, likely through salvage and reintegration, to reach the deployment goal.

## Checking Progression Steps  
- Preparing mission slots requires reserving loadout, but only one loadout is available at a time, limiting simultaneous reservations.  
- For mark_mission_node_processed, the primary slot's reserved loadout is used, while propagate_deployment doesn't directly involve loadout.  

- Applying salvage to a slot needs reserved loadout on that slot, suggesting a sequence of reserving, processing, and then salvaging.  
- Reintegrate actions release faction and salvage token but don't affect loadout, meaning loadout remains reserved after reintegration.  
- To reserve loadout for another entity, release_loadout_from_objective is needed, requiring careful sequencing to manage loadout availability.  

## Exploring Agent Progression  
- Agents follow separate paths, involving actions like installing upgrades on mission nodes, requiring committed agents and available upgrades.  
- Finalizing agent node upgrades needs reserved loadout on the agent and sets agent_node_ready, with additional steps for asset processing.  

- Agents can be assigned support units and prepared with traits based on mission requirements, adding complexity to their progression.  
- Faction markers, leadership traits, asset tokens, and tactical modifiers further influence agent readiness for promotion and deployment.  

## Analyzing Salvage Token Reuse  
- Only one salvage token (mission_token_obj) is available, initially seeming insufficient for salvaging four entities.  
- Sequencing allows reusing the salvage token: salvage one entity, reintegrate to release the token, then salvage the next, enabling all to reach deployment readiness.

## Examining Asset Paths  
- Primary slots follow a consumable path, reaching staged ready but not confirmed, while secondary slots use a support path, requiring activation and confirmation.  
- For secondary slots, activating a route needs a reserved loadout, while staging support requires committed status and assigned support.  

## Sequencing Loadout Management  
- Initializing and committing loadout on primary and secondary slots allows for staging, but releasing loadout from primary frees it for secondary use.  
- Releasing loadout from secondary after staging support makes it available again, enabling reservation on primary for mission node processing.  

## Resolving Mission Node Readiness  
- Marking a mission node processed requires the primary slot to have a reserved loadout, which was released earlier, creating a gap.  
- Re-reserving loadout on primary after secondary staging ensures mission readiness, aligning with the required preconditions.

## Extending Loadout Management  
- Finalizing primary slot activation confirms readiness and frees consumables, keeping loadout reserved.  
- Releasing loadout from primary after finalization makes it available for secondary use without affecting prepared state.  

## Finalizing Secondary Preparation  
- Secondary slot follows a similar process: initializing, reserving faction, and committing loadout before staging with consumables.  
- Finalizing secondary slot activation confirms readiness, frees consumables, and keeps loadout reserved on secondary.  

## Assembling Mission Readiness  
- Both slots, now activated and confirmed, satisfy preconditions for assembling a standard mission, setting mission_ready with assigned routes.  
- Releasing loadout from secondary makes it available again, enabling reservation on primary for node processing.  

## Processing Mission Nodes  
- Reserving loadout on primary ensures preconditions for marking mission nodes as processed, aligning with mission readiness.  
- Propagating deployment to both slots applies promotions, preparing them for salvage with mission tokens.  

## Applying Salvage Operations  
- Applying a salvage token to primary requires reserved loadout and sets salvaged status, binding the token.  
- Reintegration of primary needs reserved faction and bound salvage, progressing toward deployment readiness.

## Sequencing Agent Preparation  
- Agents require initialization before reserving faction and loadout, ensuring they’re ready for commitment, similar to slot processes.  
- Committing loadout to an agent profile, like agent_profile_obj_1, sets the stage for further upgrades and promotions.  

## Managing Faction Availability  
- Reintegrating slots releases faction options, making f1, f2, and f3 available again for agent use.  
- Agents need reserved faction options, with availability confirmed post-slot reintegration, supporting multiple agent preparations.  

## Handling Agent Upgrades and Equipment  
- Installing upgrades on mission nodes and assigning equipment to agents, like attaching with available equipment, prepares them for promotion.  
- Flagging agents for promotion requires node readiness, upgrades, and support, ensuring they meet promotion preconditions.  

## Finalizing Agent Promotion  
- Preparing agents with traits, after ensuring candidacy and upgrade status, leads to promo_ready status, ready for finalization.  
- Finalizing agent promotion without modifiers commits deployment, applying promotions and completing the agent readiness process.

## Managing Agent Promotion Flow  
- After finalizing agent promotion without modifiers, detach equipment, remove leadership traits, and unassign support from agent ap1 to free resources.  
- Apply salvage to ap1 using loadout token, then reintegrate with faction token f1, releasing the loadout afterward for reuse.  

## Sequencing Multi-Agent Operations  
- Repeat the promotion and salvage process for agent ap2, ensuring upgrades like um2 are installed and available before starting.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
